transcript: Good afternoon everybody. I'm very excited to be here today to share some of the updates from Keel. My name is Cian Breathnach. I'm the founder of Matariki Labs, contributors to Keel. Just two months ago, I had the pleasure of introducing Keel to the world at Solana Apex in Singapore. Keel is a star within the Sky or MakerDAO ecosystem. It's an onchain capital allocator focused entirely on the Solana network. At Apex, we shared Keel's big three ambitions. To bring DeFi at scale to Solana, to help improve rate stability and liquidity in Solana lending markets, and to be a catalyst for growth within the Solana RWA space. So on the first two, I'm happy to share that we've made great strides. Keel's core infrastructure went live in November, and we've integrated with our first two lending market... lending platforms to date. But today's news really focuses entirely on number three: How we start to catalyze 10x growth for RWAs on Solana. So today we officially kick off Keel's Tokenization Regatta, a public RFP for Solana native real-world assets, and a race to accelerate Solana's RWA landscape for both new and existing high-quality assets. With Season 1, we intend to deploy the first half a billion dollars of Keel's allocation capacity, and this will be the largest single placement of assets on Solana to date. So what are we looking for? The entire RFP is available on the Sky Forum for anyone who'd like to take a look. You can get there at keel.fi/regatta. But to summarize what we're looking for: High-quality tokenizations with bankruptcy-remote legal structures and thoroughly robust onchain infrastructure. USD denominated assets with low duration and no directional exposure. Highly liquid assets with a preference for daily NAV and T0 or T1 settlement. And low volatility with sustainable, risk-adjusted returns. We've split this process into two distinct tracks. Track A has a focus on assets that are already live on Solana or where issuers can make a commitment to being live and ready to receive deployments by 31st of March 2025. And Track B focuses on building a pipeline of allocation for Keel. These are quality assets in development on Solana for consideration in subsequent allocations over the next 12 to 18 months. In particular for Track B, we're working very closely with the Solana Foundation to help identify pain points and gaps that issuers are facing to try and accelerate the time to market for these high-quality assets. And I'm glad to say asset managers have really, really been leaning into this. In the run-up to the announcement today, we've had expressions of interest from more than 40 of the world's largest asset managers. And we've had dozens of providers in the tokenization stack leaning in as well. Some already support Solana issuance, but for many of these, this is the first opportunity for them to really drive the ROI and the opportunity to build deployments specifically for Solana. So why run an RFP in such a public way? Well, this is about more than just the first half a billion. The Regatta is about creating a catalyzing moment for RWAs on Solana and to enable a stocktake or a state of readiness survey across the market and identify those paths and opportunities to accelerate. Season 1's allocation will bring an immediate 60% in RWA TVL on Solana from where we stand today. But this is about opening dialogue between asset managers, between Keel, and between the Solana Foundation for many, many more issuances and allocations to come. We're very thankful to all the contributors that are making this Regatta possible: Sky, the Solana Foundation, Kinetika Research, and Particula. If you'd like to follow along, please check out keel_fi on X. And for more information on the RFP itself, you can visit that on the Sky blog by visiting keel.fi/regatta. Thank you very much.

enrichment.generated_query: Keel Product Keynote: Keel Breakpoint 2025 announcement

sources.title: Keeyu Raises $2.3M to Prevent Order Chaos During Peak Retail Season
sources.snippet:
Keeyu Raises $2.3M to Prevent Order Chaos During Peak Retail Season
* [Home](https://www.overnightsuccess.vc/)
* Posts
* Keeyu Raises $2.3M to Prevent Order Chaos During Peak Retail Season
# Keeyu Raises $2.3M to Prevent Order Chaos During Peak Retail Season
The proactive AI agent tackles $74 billion annual problem of poor post-purchase experiences in Australian e-commerce
![Will Richards](https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,quality=80,format=auto,onerror=redirect/uploads/user/profile_picture/eb9600f0-1348-46d1-97c6-f085268420bc/231109_StartUpSpeakers_07_WillRichards_0460__1_.jpg)
[Will Richards](https://www.overnightsuccess.vc/authors/eb9600f0-1348-46d1-97c6-f085268420bc)
Nov 11, 2025
Instead of building another reactive chatbot to handle complaints,[Keeyu](https://www.keeyu.com/)has developed a "proactive AI Agent" designed to monitor the entire order journey, from payment and fulfilment to delivery and returns, and prevent issues before a customer ever has to ask, "Where is my order?".
The $2.3 million round was led by**Rampersand**, with participation from**Archangel**,**Startmate**,**Empress Capital**,**Exhort Ventures**,**Sydney Angels**, and**Southern Angels**. The cap table also includes angels like Jason Wyatt (Marketplacer), Alexey Mitko (Eucalyptus), Paul Greenberg (a DealsDirect founder and early advisor to Afterpay), and Luigi Iacullo (an early investor in Canva).
❝Retailers have been stuck in reactive mode for years, waiting for customers to complain before fixing problems
Keeyu CEO and Co-Founder Jevon Le Roux
Founded in 2024 by retail and technology leaders Jevon Le Roux (CEO), Tahir Rauf (CTO), and Tracy Godtschalk (CXO), with leadership experience from P.E Nation, Sportscraft, SurfStitch, and Mastercard, saw first-hand how "disconnected systems, delayed insights, and reactive customer service" hobbled retail teams. They are betting that the real fix isn't better customer service, but preventing the need for it at its source.
“Retailers have been stuck in reactive mode for years, waiting for customers to complain before fixing problems,” said Keeyu CEO and Co-Founder Jevon Le Roux. “We built Keeyu to change that, so shoppers get what they want, on time, as promised.”
The problem is significant. During the 2024 holiday season, for example, Australia Post delivered over 103 million parcels, and many brands struggled to cope. The resulting social media backlash over late deliveries and stockouts eroded customer trust at the worst possible time , and industry studies show nearly half of customers don't return after a poor delivery experience.
### From Detection to Automation
Keeyu’s platform works by connecting a retailer's disparate systems, Shopify, ERPs, WMS, 3PLs, carriers, and returns tools into a single platform. Its AI agent monitors every order in real-time to detect issues like failed payments, warehouse delays, or stockouts.
But detection is just the first step. The company is now rolling out "Phase 2" in beta, with a public launch slated for October 2025. This next phase introduces automated issue resolution. Keeyu states the AI Agent will "not only detect problems but also automatically fix predictable ones," such as reprocessing failed payments, rerouting lost-in-transit parcels, or managing stockout communications.
Co-Founder and Chief Product Officer Tracy Godtschalk put the goal more bluntly: “Teams shouldn’t be chasing tickets. They should have the silence that comes when everything works.”
### Traction and Results
Keeyu, which only launched in July 2024, reports it has already been adopted by 15 retailers across 25 brands, including names like Decjuba, Rebel Sport NZ, Camilla, EHP Labs, Clutch Glue, and Budgy Smuggler. The company is also a finalist in the SXSW Sydney Pitch 2025 for the Future of Retail & Commerce category.
Annabel Hay, Founder and CEO of Blackbird-backed Clutch Glue and a Keeyu customer, call

# What’s new in Copilot Studio: October 2025
[Kendra Springer](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/author/kendra-springer/), Principal Group Product Manager, Microsoft Copilot Studio
![A light blue graphic design background with the following words: "What's new in Copilot Studio: October 2025".](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/CopilotStudio_WhatsNewOctober_Blog_251107-1024x576.webp)
Share
* [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/copilot-studio/whats-new-in-copilot-studio-october-2025/)
* []()
* [](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/copilot-studio/whats-new-in-copilot-studio-october-2025/)
* Content type
* [Feature Releases](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/copilot-studio/cs-content-type/feature-releases/)
* [Monthly Updates](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/copilot-studio/cs-content-type/monthly-updates/)
In this edition of our monthly roundup, we’re recapping new features released in Microsoft Copilot Studio in October 2025.
In this article
1. [Build and optimize agents](#build-and-optimize-agents)
2. [Measure and improve performance](#measure-and-improve-performance)
3. [Manage and govern at scale](#manage-and-govern-at-scale)
4. [Stay up to date on all things Copilot Studio ](#stay-up-to-date-on-all-things-copilot-studio)
In this edition of our monthly roundup, we’re recapping the most exciting new features Microsoft Copilot Studio released in October 2025.
[Try Copilot Studio today](https://signup.microsoft.com/get-started/signup?sku=606b54a9-78d8-4298-ad8b-df6ef4481c80&ru=https://copilotstudio.microsoft.com&cmpid=copilotstudio-home-hero-sta-buildchatbots&products=606b54a9-78d8-4298-ad8b-df6ef4481c80&brandingId=28b276fb-d2a0-4379-a7c0-57dce33da0f9&ali=1)
## Build and optimize agents
### Validate agents at scale with evaluations for automated testing
Agent quality just became significantly easier to measure and improve. With the automated agent evaluation experience, now available in public preview, makers can systematically test and validate their Copilot Studio agents at scale. Instead of running scenarios one by one, they can build and execute evaluation sets directly from the agent or the Test Pane, delivering structured, repeatable insights both before and after publishing.
This new experience offers flexibility in how evaluation sets are created. Makers can upload files with predefined questions and answers, reuse recent Test Pane queries, add cases manually, or instantly generate queries using AI. This approach ensures that test coverage spans organization-specific scenarios while also incorporating AI-suggested questions based on agent metadata and topics, providing a comprehensive view of performance.
![A screenshot of agent evaluations, showing a test set and match criteria.](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/1-Eval-Experience-1.webp)
Evaluations are powered by a robust grader framework that gives makers control over how accuracy is measured. Options range from strict checks such as Exact Match and Partial/Contains, to semantic comparisons like Similarity and Intent Match, and even AI-powered metrics including relevance, completeness, and groundedness. Each test delivers clear pass/fail results, detailed scores, and drill-down vi

sources.title: Upgrading Kubernetes versions just got safer with minor version rollback
sources.snippet:
Kubernetes gets minor version rollback | Google Cloud Blog
[Jump to Content](./#content)
[
Cloud](https://cloud.google.com/)

[

Containers & Kubernetes
# Upgrading Kubernetes versions just got safer with minor version rollback
November 4, 2025
* []()
* []()
* []()
* []()
##### Siyuan Zhang
Software Engineer
##### Wenjia Zhang
Engineering Manager
##### Try Gemini 3
Our most intelligent model is now available on Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise
[Try now](https://console.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/studio/multimodal)
Upgrading a Kubernetes cluster has always been a one-way street: you move forward, and if the control plane has an issue, your only option is to roll forward with a fix. This adds significant risk to routine maintenance, a problem made worse as organizations upgrade more frequently for new AI features while demanding maximum reliability. Today, in partnership with the Kubernetes community, we are introducing a new capability in Kubernetes 1.33 that solves this: Kubernetes control-plane minor-version rollback. For the first time, you have a reliable path to revert a control-plane upgrade, fundamentally changing cluster lifecycle management.This feature is available in open-source Kubernetes, and is integrated and generally available in Google Kubernetes Engine starting in GKE 1.33 soon.
### **The challenge: Why were rollbacks so hard?**
Kubernetes' control plane components, especially kube-apiserver and etcd, are stateful and highly sensitive to API version changes. When you upgrade, many new APIs and features are introduced in the new binary. Some data might be migrated to new formats and API versions. Downgrading was unsupported because there was no mechanism to safely revert changes, risking data corruption and complete cluster failure.
As a simple example, consider adding a new field to an existing resource. Until now, both the storage and API progressed in a single step, allowing clients to write data to that new field immediately. If a regression was detected, rolling back removed access to that field, but the data written to it would not be garbage-collected. Instead, it would persist silently in etcd. This left the administrator in an impossible situation. Worse, upon a future re-upgrade to that minor version, this stale "garbage" data could suddenly become "alive" again, introducing potentially problematic and indeterministic behavior.
### **The solution: Emulated versions**
The Kubernetes Enhancement Proposal (KEP),[KEP-4330: Compatibility Versions](https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-architecture/4330-compatibility-versions), introduces the concept of an "emulated version" for the control plane. Contributed by Googlers, this creates a new two-step upgrade process:
* **Step 1: Upgrade binaries.**You upgrade the control plane binary, but the "emulated version" stays the same as the pre-upgrade version. At this stage, all APIs, features, and storage data formats remain unchanged. This makes it safe to roll back your control plane to the previously stable version if you find a problem.
* **Validate health and check for regressions.**The 1st step creates a safe validation window during which you can verify that it's safe to proceed —for example, making sure your own components or workloads are running healthy under the new binaries and checking for any performance regressions before committing to the new API versions.
* **Step 2:****Finalize upgrade.**After you complete your testing, you "bump" the emulated version to the new version. This enables all the new APIs and features of the latest Kubernetes release and completes the upgrade.

editorial.headline: Sky’s Keel Allocates $500M to Solana Real-World Assets
editorial.standfirst: The capital allocator for the Sky ecosystem launches a 'Tokenization Regatta' to deploy half a billion dollars into Solana-native credit and treasury products. The initiative marks the largest single asset placement in the network's history.
key_takeaways: Keel will deploy $500M in 'Season 1,' targeting a 60% immediate uplift in Solana’s RWA TVL.
The 'Regatta' RFP mandates bankruptcy-remote, USD-denominated assets with T0/T1 settlement.
Over 40 asset managers have expressed interest, with deployments targeting Q1 2026.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The institutional bridge to Solana just widened significantly. **Keel**, the autonomous allocator entity within the **Sky** (formerly **MakerDAO**) ecosystem, has announced a $500 million capital injection targeting Solana-native real-world assets (RWAs). Dubbed the "Tokenization Regatta," this public RFP is not a grant program but a direct liquidity deployment designed to force-mature the network's asset issuance standards. By acting as a massive buy-side anchor, **Keel** is effectively bootstrapping a high-liquidity RWA market, with the initial tranche alone projected to increase Solana’s RWA Total Value Locked (TVL) by 60%.

**Matariki Labs** founder **Cian Breathnach** outlined strict criteria for the allocation, signaling a move away from experimental pilots toward institutional-grade plumbing. Eligible assets must be bankruptcy-remote, USD-denominated, and capable of T0 or T1 settlement. The initiative is split into two tracks: Track A for assets ready for immediate deployment by March 31, 2026, and Track B to cultivate a pipeline for the subsequent 12 to 18 months. This structure allows **Keel** to deploy capital rapidly while simultaneously guiding issuers through the compliance and technical hurdles required for the **Sky** ecosystem.

This aggressive deployment suggests **Sky** is betting heavily on Solana's high-throughput architecture to handle the next generation of DeFi credit. With over 40 global asset managers already signaling interest, the "Regatta" serves as a stress test for issuers and a signal that Solana is ready to host complex, yield-bearing financial instruments at scale. The process is being supported by the **Solana Foundation**, **Kinetika Research**, and **Particula**, ensuring that the selected assets meet the rigorous risk parameters required to back **Sky**'s decentralized stablecoin liquidity.
